name: Greedy Algorithm
def: Builds a solution by repeatedly taking the locally optimal choice.
problem: Reach a global optimum cheaply when local optimal choices provably compose to it.
mechanism: At each step pick the best immediate option per a selection rule; correctness requires the greedy-choice property and optimal substructure (often proven via matroids/exchange arguments).
benefits: Very fast, often O(n log n)
limits: Only correct when the greedy property holds
Easy to apply incorrectly and get suboptimal results
failures: Confidently wrong answers when greedy property fails
No backtracking to recover from a bad early choice
whenNot: Problems needing global search (use DP/branch & bound)
When the greedy property is unproven
myths: "Greedy is just a heuristic" — for matroids it is provably optimal
